intent: Audit the consumable-certificate framing, disclose inactive or unresolved causal links, and propose upstream revision without confirming G1.

entity.title: Economic audit of consumable certificate framing
entity.summary: The stock and composition puzzle is retellable, but certification semantics, active payoff margins, transition law, benchmark implementation, and selection remain unresolved.

tension.conventional_prediction: A lower certification cost should make certification privately cheaper and may contemporaneously reduce reliance on inspection.
tension.economic_puzzle: The proposed stock channel could make a contemporaneous cheaper-certification incentive differ from the dynamic information environment, but the candidate does not yet define a closed game in which that distinction is operative.
tension.resolution_target: Determine whether a well-defined transition and active seller margin make consumption and uncertified composition necessary for any change in inspection, allocation, or buyer surplus as k varies.

forces.label: Visible-certificate direct-purchase force
forces.operative_margin: A buyer compares direct purchase from a valid visible certificate with paying c to inspect an uncertified seller.
forces.economic_logic: A valid certificate supplies a high-success direct option and can reduce the buyer's value of inspection in the same public state.
forces.active_when: At least one valid certificate is visible, c>0, and direct purchase from that seller is feasible.

forces.label: Consumption and uncertified-composition force
forces.operative_margin: Trade-triggered depletion changes the public stock and may change the posterior for an uncertified seller.
forces.economic_logic: The stock can affect which seller is uncertified and therefore the informational return to inspection; the payoff relevance of upstream certification remains unresolved.
forces.active_when: No valid certificate is visible, inspection is feasible, and the uncertified posterior is interior.

causal_chain.cause: The public certificate stock changes the posterior faced by a buyer considering an uncertified seller.
causal_chain.endogenous_response: The buyer compares inspection with direct purchase and exit in the resulting public state.
causal_chain.consequence: The buyer's inspection choice may change.

causal_chain.cause: The uncertified-pool posterior changes the payoff to paying c for inspection.
causal_chain.endogenous_response: Inspection is chosen when its continuation-inclusive payoff weakly exceeds the best direct-purchase or exit alternative.
causal_chain.consequence: Inspection changes the buyer's trade or exit action.

causal_chain.cause: The inspection decision determines whether the buyer trades conditionally on observed capacity or follows the direct-purchase alternative.
causal_chain.endogenous_response: The buyer's trade or exit action changes, with no direction asserted for allocation or surplus.
causal_chain.consequence: The realized buyer action and service outcome may differ.

minimal_example.title: Certificate-visible state versus zero-certificate state
minimal_example.setup: There are two sellers and one buyer. In state z=10 seller 1 has a valid truthful certificate; in state z=00 neither seller has one. Buying is free, successful service gives value one, and inspection costs c>0.
minimal_example.moving_primitive: The experiment varies k and therefore potentially the frequency of z=00, subject to a completed certification and transition law.
held_fixed: buyer information at each public state
endogenous_responses: inspection only in states where it is not dominated
minimal_example.predicted_pattern: In z=10, direct purchase of the certified seller yields one, while inspecting an uncertified seller and then choosing optimally costs c and cannot exceed one before that cost. Thus inspection is inactive there; no sign for the effect of k is asserted.
minimal_example.economic_intuition: Any aggregate search response can therefore come from the frequency of zero-certificate states and from the conditional response inside those states, not from inspection competing with a valid certificate in the same state.
minimal_example.limitation: The example does not establish equilibrium existence, stationary weights, welfare, or non-monotonicity.
cannot_establish: the sign of any comparative static

economist_memo.headline: Consumable certificates may alter the state distribution, but the current framing has not yet established an active closed-loop mechanism.
economist_memo.opening_question: Can lowering k change inspection, allocation, or buyer surplus through certificates that are visible until trade and then consumed?
economist_memo.benchmark_message: The intended contrasts remove consumption, freeze uncertified composition, or freeze inspection, but their common state/action interpretation and equilibrium status are not yet secured.
economist_memo.tension_message: The mechanical cheaper-certification force points toward more certification, while depletion could change later uncertified states; the direction is unresolved.
economist_memo.mechanism_message: The current primitive graph supplies a verbal stock-to-composition path, but it omits a closed transition law and an established payoff-active certification margin.
economist_memo.result_preview: The audit predicts a revision requirement and records a dominance boundary: valid visible certificates make inspection unnecessary when c>0 under the literal ledger.
economist_memo.contribution_message: No novelty, proof, validation, welfare, or mechanism-distinctiveness claim is made.
economist_memo.scope_condition: Any future claim requires explicit certificate semantics, public-state transitions, Bayes updating, complete actions and payoffs, and a stated equilibrium/selection convention.
economist_memo.reader_takeaway: The economically defensible interim claim is a proposed state-distribution question, not yet a demonstrated feedback result.

held_fixed.label: service value, seller margin, and discounting

benchmark_assessments.channel_summary: The focal path is proposed but not active-identified because the seller payoff margin and transition law are unresolved.

aggregate_invariance.basis: No aggregate invariance is claimed; the stock is itself a proposed state variable.
aggregate_invariance.implication_for_attribution: Aggregate attribution is unavailable until the weighting distribution and transition law are specified.

selection_assurance.selection_rule: No equilibrium concept or selector is specified upstream.
selection_assurance.basis: The candidate labels a dynamic Bayesian equilibrium candidate but does not define selection.
selection_assurance.implication_for_attribution: Any mechanism attribution must remain qualified or unresolved.

benchmark_assessments.attribution_basis: The focal channel is a hypothesis, not a demonstrated active mechanism.

distinctive_mechanism.basis: The contrast is intended to remove depletion, but certificate semantics and an active margin are not established.

benchmark_assessments.channel_summary: This is a transition contrast, but it is not yet a valid mechanism control because persistent certificate validity is undefined.

aggregate_invariance.implication_for_attribution: The benchmark cannot support aggregate attribution before the transition is closed.

selection_assurance.basis: The same selection convention is not yet defined across the two transition laws.

benchmark_assessments.attribution_basis: The changed transition is intended to isolate depletion, but semantics may also change.

distinctive_mechanism.basis: The contrast may alter certificate meaning, not only depletion.

benchmark_assessments.channel_summary: This is a diagnostic frozen-posterior comparison, not yet a Bayes-consistent equilibrium benchmark.

aggregate_invariance.basis: Freezing a posterior does not freeze state weights or policies.
aggregate_invariance.implication_for_attribution: No aggregate composition claim is supported.

selection_assurance.selection_rule: The imposed posterior is an exogenous convention rather than an equilibrium selector.
selection_assurance.basis: The benchmark does not specify how the fixed posterior is generated from observed histories.
selection_assurance.implication_for_attribution: Attribution must be downgraded to diagnostic-only.

benchmark_assessments.attribution_basis: The benchmark changes an informational object directly and may not preserve the same Bayesian game.

distinctive_mechanism.basis: No distinctive mechanism contribution is claimed from an imposed posterior.

benchmark_assessments.channel_summary: This freezes a buyer policy and therefore is a policy counterfactual, not an equilibrium benchmark with the same optimization problem.

aggregate_invariance.basis: The inspection policy may be pointwise fixed while stock-dependent weighting remains endogenous.
aggregate_invariance.implication_for_attribution: No aggregate invariance follows from the frozen rule.

selection_assurance.selection_rule: Inspection is imposed by an exogenous rule.
selection_assurance.basis: A fixed rule is not equilibrium selection robustness.
selection_assurance.implication_for_attribution: The comparison can diagnose the inspection margin only, not establish an equilibrium result.

benchmark_assessments.attribution_basis: The benchmark changes strategic optimization by imposing a policy.

distinctive_mechanism.basis: No distinctive mechanism contribution is claimed from an imposed buyer rule.

disclosed_gaps.description: A certificate persists until trade while capacity is described as current and privately observed; the certified object is not defined across periods.
disclosed_gaps.consequence: The stock state and non-consumption contrast may not describe the same information object.
disclosed_gaps.resolution_needed: Choose locked-unit certification or current-period certification and rewrite the transition and benchmarks.

disclosed_gaps.description: The public state, seller certification action, buyer post-inspection actions, failure payoff, payment convention, and continuation-inclusive payoffs are not closed.
disclosed_gaps.consequence: No active best response, stationary distribution, or equilibrium correspondence can be assessed.
disclosed_gaps.resolution_needed: Add a state/action/payoff table and an exact transition kernel before claiming an active link.

disclosed_gaps.description: With a valid certificate, free direct purchase and buyer value one, inspection costs c and cannot improve on the certified seller.
disclosed_gaps.consequence: Inspection is inactive in certificate-visible states for c>0, so the claimed inspection-to-depletion feedback is not established.
disclosed_gaps.resolution_needed: Record the dominance boundary and either restrict search to zero-certificate states or add a substantive price, mismatch, rationing, or certificate-validity friction.

disclosed_gaps.description: The fixed-posterior and exogenous-inspection rows alter beliefs or optimization without specifying a common equilibrium state/action interpretation; no mechanical k-only placebo is listed.
disclosed_gaps.consequence: Benchmark differences cannot be attributed uniquely to stock depletion, composition, endogenous inspection, or cheaper certification.
disclosed_gaps.resolution_needed: Use common states/actions and change one transition, belief kernel, policy constraint, or accounting term at a time; add an explicit k-only placebo.

disclosed_gaps.description: No equilibrium selector, ergodicity condition, invariant weighting convention, or selection robustness is specified.
disclosed_gaps.consequence: Pointwise policy statements cannot be promoted to aggregate inspection, allocation, or surplus claims.
disclosed_gaps.resolution_needed: Specify a selector or state that only conditional diagnostics will be reported; define aggregate weights after the transition is closed.

payload.action_rationale: The puzzle is economically intelligible and the intended benchmark contrasts are visible, but the active certification margin, certificate semantics, transition law, dominance boundary, benchmark implementation, and aggregate/selection basis remain unresolved. The audit therefore proposes upstream revision and records no human G1 action.

entity.title: Audited proposal for G1 revision
entity.summary: The strengthened dossier records economic gaps and proposes revision; it does not confirm G1.

requirements.description: The puzzle, opposing force, illustrative dominance boundary, and proposed state-distribution channel are retellable without a sign, novelty, proof, validation, or welfare claim.
The seller certification payoff witness is unresolved because the upstream action node, continuation law, and complete payoff ledger are absent; no inactive link is relabeled as a later scope choice.
For c>0 and a valid visible certificate, inspection is disclosed as dominated under the literal value and cost ledger; any remaining search channel must be state-occupancy or require a new friction.
All four benchmark IDs are audited exactly once, with changes, fixed objects, reoptimization, endogenous objects, channel paths, aggregate status, and selection status.
Certificate semantics, transition law, payoff closure, benchmark implementation, and aggregate/selection basis remain unresolved and block readiness; no human G1 action is recorded or implied.

payload.rationale: The audit supports a precise revision agenda but not G1 readiness. It records no human confirmation, promotion, or scope decision.

outcome.rationale: The audit creates a new FramingQualityBundle and strengthened replacement G1 dossier, records the unresolved certification margin, certificate semantics, dominance boundary, transition and benchmark defects, and proposes revision without recording or implying a human G1 decision.
